channel.title: Others on Jin
channel.description: Recent content in Others on Jin

item.title: Afternoon Tea
item.description:
Sensory snippet written for the colorwheel project by Wild Violet Fibers, a small hand-dyed yarn business.

Official online listings: London Fog | Afternoon Tea

Afternoon Tea

Afternoon sun filtered through swaying, sea-blue whispers of melamine beads and aged linen. Every day, from late morning to evening, golden light drenched the space between these curtains, steeping its dedicated resident in daylong warmth.

Ms. Snickers was sprawled comfortably over the periwinkle table runner, seeming to melt into the table as sunlight spread like sweetened malt through her whole being. Her whiskered, tan face was tucked neatly behind two curled paws. Her only movements were the gentle tremble of her pale, generous sides as she purred, and the occasional flick of her brown-tufted ears scattering the steam from a nearby teacup.

item.title: London Fog
item.description:
Sensory snippet written for the colorwheel project by Wild Violet Fibers, a small hand-dyed yarn business.

Official online listings: London Fog | Afternoon Tea

London Fog

It’s just shy of dawn.

You can scarce see any lamplight past all the bruise-deep darkness and fog. Still, you plod through the mist. Silvery autumnal chill scrapes over your nose and into your lungs, quickly smothering the white staccato plume of your breath.

Further away from central London you go. Over old leaves heavily creased with the late season, the feather-thin puddles reflecting dreary skies. Past Snaresbrook and Magpie Hill, all the way until the trees’ shadows grow long, ancient, tinged with rime. Damp wood creaks wearily beneath your boots, and your every step ripples across the lake in echoes of mauve. It takes some time for the ripples to settle, and even then, the forest waters are never truly still.

item.title: Social media post example
item.description:
This is a draft post to be used in Wild Violet Fibers’ social media accounts, email marketing letter, and online Jira submission form.

  • Wild Violet Fibers, is promoting collaborative short story projects.

  • To keep the process more organized going forward, the business has adopted some changes to help with tracking progress between multiple writers, the business owner, and the new editor-in-chief.

Colorwheel Project - Call for Writers Form

Hello writers!

We have a new batch of short story slots open for the Colorwheel project. Please feel free to check out some of the new short stories that have been published there!
